nodes.label: The Naughty Boy (Poem)
nodes.description: A poem by John Keats about a boy who runs away to Scotland

nodes.label: The Boy's Journey
nodes.description: Runs from England to Scotland expecting everything to be different

nodes.label: His Discovery
nodes.description: Scotland is not so different -- ground is hard, sun is hot, just like home

nodes.label: Message: Be Content
nodes.description: Be happy with what you have; the grass is not always greener elsewhere

nodes.label: Pinocchio (Story)
nodes.description: The tale of a wooden puppet who wants to become a real boy

nodes.label: Geppetto the Carpenter
nodes.description: A kind old man who carves Pinocchio from wood

nodes.label: The Growing Nose
nodes.description: Pinocchio's nose grows longer every time he tells a lie

nodes.label: Lessons Learned
nodes.description: Be honest, go to school, listen to elders, be kind

nodes.label: Becoming a Real Boy
nodes.description: Good behaviour and honesty help Pinocchio become real

nodes.label: Grammar: Punctuation
nodes.description: Using marks to make writing clear and meaningful

nodes.label: Full Stop (.)
nodes.description: Ends a statement: 'Pinocchio is a puppet.'

nodes.label: Exclamation (!) & Comma (,)
nodes.description: ! for strong feelings: 'How wonderful!' Comma for pauses and lists
